findings.format_error_reduction: Structured Outputs can reduce malformed extraction output by enforcing a supplied JSON schema instead of relying on prompt-only JSON.
findings.semantic_completeness: Schema adherence does not prove that extracted EvidenceSpans cover every critical scanner finding; deterministic scanner coverage remains authoritative.
findings.latency: Provider-side schema processing and constrained decoding can add latency and first-request schema preprocessing cost, so production use needs separate measurement.
findings.failure_modes: Unsupported strict-mode schema keywords, model refusal, incomplete outputs, provider errors, or schema incompatibility must fail closed before local validation.
findings.fallback_behavior: Fallback must be local validation plus clarification or human review; the system must not silently downgrade to non-strict JSON for authority escalation.
findings.provider_boundary_risks: Provider outputs are extraction proposals only and must not grant tools, scopes, validators, policies, or broader runtime authority.
